slides.title: Starting a Session
slides.content:
# session_start()

To use sessions, you must call `session_start()` at the beginning of your script (before any output). This function:

*   Starts a new session or resumes an existing one.
*   Populates `$_SESSION` with the session data.

```php
<?php
session_start();
?>
```

After calling it, you can read and write `$_SESSION` like a normal array.

slides.title: Storing Data in Session
Headers Already Sent
slides.content:
## Common Error: Headers Already Sent

`session_start()` must be called before any HTML output. If you see "headers already sent" errors, ensure there is no whitespace or echo before `session_start()`.

slides.title: Checking if Session Variable Exists
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
session_start();
if (isset($_SESSION['username'])) {
    echo "Hello, " . $_SESSION['username'];
} else {
    echo "Please log in.";
}
?>

quizData.question: What must you call before using $_SESSION?
